name: Tour of Biscay: Urdaibai Coast Loop
name_original: Elanchove y Ea – circular desde Gautéguiz de Arteaga
description: This difficult 21.6-mile touring cycling route from Gautéguiz de Arteaga explores coastal villages and the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Gautegiz Arteaga navigate a landscape characterized by the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, featuring coastal stretches, river valleys, and rolling hills. The area includes a mix of natural scenery and historic towns. Elevations are present, offering varied gradients for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Gautegiz Arteaga The most popular touring cycling route is Tour of Biscay: Urdaibai Coast Loop, a 21.6 miles (34.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Urdaibai coast. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Guernica Mural in Gernika – Artzubi Bridge loop from San Kristobal, a difficult 35.6 miles (57.3 km) path. This route passes through cultural landmarks and river valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Guernica Mural in Gernika – Gernika loop from Busturia - San Kristobal, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail leading through historic towns and scenic countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Gautegiz Arteaga is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and historic towns. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Gautegiz Arteaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1,600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Gautegiz Arteaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes in the Gautegiz Arteaga area, offering a wide range of options for exploring the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve and its surroundings.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: The routes around Gautegiz Arteaga cater to various skill levels. While the majority, over 80, are considered difficult due to the region's rolling hills and elevations, there are also around 25 moderate routes and a few easy options available for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: Yes, there are a few easy touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga, perfect for beginners or families looking for a gentle ride. These routes typically follow flatter terrain along river valleys or coastal paths, offering a pleasant experience without significant climbs.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: Touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga offer diverse scenery, from the coastal stretches and marshlands of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve to lush river valleys and historic towns. You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Gautegiz Arteaga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068624703/tour-of-biscay-urdaibai-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay: Urdaibai Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option offering coastal views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by significant points of interest. You might cycle past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1923359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzubi Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views over the Urdaibai marsh. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Katillotxu Dolmen – Mundaka Harbor loop from Busturia Itsasbegi&lt;/a&gt; also takes you past interesting sites.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling in Gautegiz Arteaga, with milder temperatures and less rainfall. Summer can be warm, especially inland, while winter can bring cooler, wetter weather, though cycling is possible year-round.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region's varied terrain means many routes include climbs that reward cyclists with panoramic vistas. Look for routes that pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking scenery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 70 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the blend of natural landscapes with historic towns, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs that characterize the region.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through or near historic towns?
A: Yes, several routes are designed to explore the cultural heritage of the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40325434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Gernika loop from Busturia - San Kristobal&lt;/a&gt; leads through historic towns and scenic countryside, allowing you to experience the local culture.

Q: What should I consider packing for a touring bike trip in Gautegiz Arteaga?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, it's advisable to pack layers, including waterproof gear. A repair kit, sufficient water, and snacks are essential. For routes with climbs, consider your gearing. Don't forget a camera for the scenic views!

Q: Are there options for moderate touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, alongside the more challenging routes, there are approximately 25 moderate touring cycling routes around Gautegiz Arteaga. These routes offer a good balance of distance and elevation, suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a rewarding ride without extreme difficulty. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Artzubi Bridge loop from Busturia - San Kristobal&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The castle of Arteaga, also known as the castle of Empress Eugenia de Montijo, was built in the 19th century in neo-medieval style on the foundations of an 8th century castle. Its location stands out, in a large flat meadow surrounded by vegetation, unlike the medieval defensive castles built on a hill.
The Urdaibai marshes take you to enjoy one of the most beautiful routes in Vizcaya.

The absence of populations and the natural environment give Laga beach a wild vibe during the non-summer months. A perfect place for a stop and a swim, if you can stand the fresh water of the Bay of Biscay.

Picturesque fishing village on the Basque coast, known for its unique location on a steep hillside that slopes down to the sea. The town, with old buildings and a beautiful church, offers impressive views of the Bay of Biscay.

Ea is a small Basque town that has several stone coves protected by huge cliffs and lush vegetation. Highly coveted spaces among locals and tourists during the summer season. The town itself is covered in a very short time.
This is a beautiful mountain route on roads with moderate traffic. My experiences were good.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Elanchove y Ea touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the route takes around 2 hours and 37 minutes. However, as a difficult touring cycling route with significant elevation changes, especially if you're carrying luggage or prefer a more leisurely pace to enjoy the scenery, you should plan for a longer duration.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of paved roads, making it suitable for road touring bikes. However, be prepared for undulating terrain with significant elevation gains and descents. It will test your endurance with challenging climbs typical of coastal and inland regional roads in the Basque Country.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is officially rated as 'difficult' and requires a very good level of fitness due to its significant elevation gain (792 meters) over a moderate distance (34.8 kilometers). It is best suited for experienced touring cyclists.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the Elanchove y Ea cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of coastal and inland beauty. Highlights include the historic Arteaga Castle, the stunning Urdaibai Marshes, and the beautiful Laga Beach. You'll also pass through charming fishing villages like Elantxobe and Ea, offering quintessential Basque coastal views.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start the route in Gautéguiz de Arteaga?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Gautéguiz de Arteaga. While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, you should look for public parking areas within the town to begin your cycling tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Basque Country generally has mild weather. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential winter rains. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route or to access the general areas of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, as it is a protected natural environment, please respect all local regulations regarding waste, noise, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on paved roads, it's a touring cycling route, not a hiking trail. If you plan to bring a dog, it would need to be carried in a suitable bike trailer or basket for its safety and the safety of others, as dogs are not typically allowed to run alongside bicycles on public roads. Always ensure your dog is safe and comfortable, and adhere to local leash laws in towns.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other paths. Notably, it covers about 53% of the Bizkaiko bira trail. You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with trails like Atxarre. Artadiaren bihotzaren erdian and Erregeen bidetik.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns and villages where you can find amenities. Elantxobe and Ea are charming fishing ports that offer opportunities for refreshments and meals. Gautéguiz de Arteaga, your starting point, also has facilities.

faqs.question: What makes the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve special for cyclists on this route?
faqs.answer: Cycling through the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve offers a unique experience. As a UNESCO protected area, you'll be immersed in rich biodiversity and impressive landscapes, from the expansive Urdaibai Marshes to stunning coastal views and verdant countryside. It's a chance to connect with a protected natural environment while enjoying a challenging ride.
